thinking_about: Lawrence Weiskrantz coined blindsight in 1974 and spent decades with patient GY. When pressed to describe what he experienced in his blind field, GY said: it is more an awareness but you don’t see it. He found a crack between two things that language usually treats as one. Whether the third state is genuine phenomenology or retrospective inference is unsettled. But the precision of the sentence — the fact that it’s hard to say — is evidence that it’s pointing at something real.
